criteria.priority_notes: Standard RIA evaluation criterion. The topic scope emphasises novelty in AI agent architecture including autonomous planning and reasoning, advanced memory management, multi-agent frameworks, and multimodal capabilities as key technical differentiators. No topic-specific weight is stated in the source documents; standard Horizon Europe RIA weighting must be verified against Tier 2A evaluation form templates and Tier 1 General Annexes.
criteria.assumption_note: The Excellence criterion and its standard weight (typically 50% for RIA per Horizon Europe General Annexes) are not stated in the topic source documents (slice.json or curated extract). This entry is derived from the general Horizon Europe RIA evaluation framework applicable to the instrument type recorded in selected_call.json. Weight and criterion definition must be verified against Tier 1 General Annexes and Tier 2A RIA evaluation form templates.

criteria.priority_notes: Topic source documents signal the following impact-relevant priorities for evaluators: (1) contribution to the Apply AI Strategy; (2) alignment with and cohesion activities for the ADRA Partnership; (3) sector coverage positioning across healthcare, advanced manufacturing, and in-vehicle autonomous driving (portfolio selection procedure); (4) sharing of communicable results via the AI-on-demand platform; (5) linkage to AI Factories, TEFs, and EDIHs for validation and deployment.
criteria.inference_note: The Impact criterion name is assumed from the standard Horizon Europe RIA evaluation framework and is not stated in the topic source documents. The topic-specific impact priorities listed in priority_notes are derived from explicit scope, partnership, and reporting requirements in the source documents and are expected to be evaluated under the Impact criterion. Weight (typically 30% for RIA) must be verified against Tier 1 General Annexes and Tier 2A evaluation form templates.

criteria.priority_notes: Topic source documents signal the following implementation-quality priorities for evaluators: (1) mandatory qualitative and quantitative KPIs, benchmarking, and progress monitoring mechanisms; (2) mandatory cohesion activities with ADRA and the GenAI4EU central Hub; (3) collaboration with relevant international, European, national, and regional initiatives; (4) reuse of public results from previously funded actions where possible.
criteria.inference_note: The Quality and Efficiency of Implementation criterion name is assumed from the standard Horizon Europe RIA evaluation framework and is not stated in the topic source documents. The topic-specific implementation requirements listed in priority_notes are explicitly stated in the source documents and are expected to be evaluated under this criterion. Weight (typically 20% for RIA) must be verified against Tier 1 General Annexes and Tier 2A evaluation form templates.

criteria.priority_notes: Grants will be awarded not only in order of ranking score but also to ensure at least one funded project in each of three sectors: healthcare, advanced manufacturing, and in-vehicle autonomous driving. This is a post-threshold award selection criterion applied after individual evaluation scores are determined; it is not a standard evaluation sub-criterion with a numeric weight and does not replace the standard Excellence, Impact, and Quality and Efficiency of Implementation criteria.
